title: Weak Passwords and Authentication Methods
description: Learn how weak passwords can lead to unauthorized database access.

attack_stages.title: Weak Passwords
attack_stages.description: The organization allows weak or predictable passwords.

attack_stages.title: Unauthorized Login
attack_stages.description: An attacker successfully logs into a legitimate user account.

attack_stages.title: Database Access
attack_stages.description: The attacker reaches sensitive database resources.

attack_stages.title: Business Impact
attack_stages.description: The organization suffers operational, financial, or reputational damage.

prevention_points.title: Enforce Strong Password Policies
prevention_points.description: Require long, complex, and unique passwords.

prevention_points.title: Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
prevention_points.description: Require a second authentication factor before granting access.

prevention_points.title: Disable Default Credentials
prevention_points.description: Replace factory-default usernames and passwords immediately.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A company stores employee information in an internal database. Employees log in daily using a username and password.

nodes.title: Weakness Detected
nodes.text: Several employees use short and predictable passwords. The system only requires a password for authentication.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to gain unauthorized access to the employee database.

nodes.title: What weakness makes this attack possible?
Try Again
nodes.text: That option would make the attack more difficult. Look for the actual weakness.

nodes.title: Unauthorized Login
nodes.text: Because weak passwords are used, the attacker successfully logs into an employee account.

nodes.title: Database Access
nodes.text: The attacker now has access to confidential employee records stored in the database.

nodes.title: Sensitive Data Exposure
nodes.text: Employee information can now be viewed, copied, modified, or stolen without authorization.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should require strong passwords, implement multi-factor authentication, eliminate default credentials, and regularly review password policies.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review the path the attacker followed and identify where the attack could have been stopped.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: You have completed this scenario. Remember that security is strongest when multiple layers of protection work together.
